code: # Create CohortDef objects for your cohorts base_cohort <- CohortDef$new(   label = "Type 2 Diabetes Base",   tags = list(category = "base", domain = "condition"),   filePath = "inputs/cohorts/json/type2dm.json" )  hypertension <- CohortDef$new(   label = "Hypertension",   tags = list(category = "base", domain = "condition"),   filePath = "inputs/cohorts/json/hypertension.json" )  ckd <- CohortDef$new(   label = "Chronic Kidney Disease",   tags = list(category = "base", domain = "condition"),   filePath = "inputs/cohorts/json/ckd.json" )  # Create manifest with all cohorts exec_settings <- createExecutionSettings(   connectionDetails = connection_details,   cdmDatabaseSchema = "cdm.dbo",   workDatabaseSchema = "results.dbo",   tempEmulationSchema = NULL,   cohortTable = "cohort",   databaseName = "optum_dod" )  cohort_manifest <- CohortManifest$new(   cohortEntries = list(base_cohort, hypertension, ckd),   executionSettings = exec_settings,   dbPath = "inputs/cohorts/cohort_manifest.sqlite" )  # View all cohorts in manifest manifest_df <- cohort_manifest$tabulateManifest() print(manifest_df)

previous_headings: Step 4: Create Analysis Tasks and Supporting Code
title: Developing the Pipeline
text: parameterized SQL queries used SqlRender, create files analysis/src/sql/ using makeSrcSqlFile(): creates standardized SQL file analysis/src/sql/drug_exposure_summary.sql placeholders SqlRender parameters. Important: SqlRender uses @paramName notation substitution. ’s realistic example builds intermediate work table multi-step logic: Best Practice: Wrap SQL queries utility functions stored analysis/src/ files, call functions tasks. keeps SQL execution logic organized reusable: call function task file: Workflow Summary: 1. SQL file creates intermediate work table computed values 2. R function orchestrates render → translate → execute → collect 3. Task file sources utility function gets back dataframe 4. database configuration comes ExecutionSettings
code: makeSrcSqlFile(   fileName = "drug_exposure_summary",   author = "Jane Doe",   description = "Create a summary table of drug exposure by observation period" ) /*  Document your parameters at the top:   @cdmDatabaseSchema - Schema containing OMOP CDM tables   @workDatabaseSchema - Schema for output tables   @workTableName - Name of the intermediate work table to create   @studyStartDate - Start date for filtering   @studyEndDate - End date for filtering */  -- Create intermediate table of drug exposures by observation period CREATE TABLE @workDatabaseSchema.@workTableName AS  WITH obs_periods AS (   -- Get all observation periods for study participants   SELECT      person_id,     observation_period_start_date,     observation_period_end_date   FROM @cdmDatabaseSchema.observation_period   WHERE observation_period_start_date >= '@studyStartDate'     AND observation_period_start_date <= '@studyEndDate' ),  drug_exp AS (   -- Get drug exposures during these observation periods   SELECT     d.person_id,     op.observation_period_start_date,     op.observation_period_end_date,     d.drug_concept_id,     d.drug_exposure_start_date,     COALESCE(d.drug_exposure_end_date, d.drug_exposure_start_date) AS drug_exposure_end_date,     COALESCE(d.days_supply, 1) AS days_supply   FROM @cdmDatabaseSchema.drug_exposure d   JOIN obs_periods op     ON d.person_id = op.person_id     AND d.drug_exposure_start_date >= op.observation_period_start_date     AND d.drug_exposure_start_date <= op.observation_period_end_date ),  person_summary AS (   -- Aggregate to person-level summaries   SELECT     person_id,     observation_period_start_date,     observation_period_end_date,     COUNT(DISTINCT drug_concept_id) AS num_unique_drugs,     COUNT(DISTINCT drug_exposure_start_date) AS num_drug_exposures,     SUM(days_supply) AS total_days_supply,     AVG(days_supply) AS avg_days_supply,     MIN(drug_exposure_start_date) AS first_drug_date,     MAX(drug_exposure_end_date) AS last_drug_date   FROM drug_exp   GROUP BY person_id, observation_period_start_date, observation_period_end_date )  SELECT * FROM person_summary; # Example utility function in analysis/src/execute_sql_queries.R  get_drug_exposure_summary <- function(executionSettings,                                       workTableName = "drug_exposure_summary",                                       studyStartDate = "2020-01-01",                                       studyEndDate = "2023-12-31") {   # Read the SQL file   sql <- readr::read_file(here::here("analysis/src/sql/drug_exposure_summary.sql"))      # Step 1: Render parameters using SqlRender   rendered_drug_sql <- SqlRender::render(     sql,     cdmDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$cdmDatabaseSchema,     workDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$workDatabaseSchema,     workTableName = workTableName,     studyStartDate = studyStartDate,     studyEndDate = studyEndDate   )      # Step 2: Translate to DBMS dialect   # The ExecutionSettings object knows your DBMS type and tempEmulationSchema   translated_drug_sql <- SqlRender::translate(     rendered_drug_sql,     targetDialect = executionSettings$getDbms(),     tempEmulationSchema = executionSettings$tempEmulationSchema   )      # Get the connection from ExecutionSettings   connection <- executionSettings$getConnection()      # If connection is null, establish one   if (is.null(connection)) {     executionSettings$connect()     connection <- executionSettings$getConnection()   }      # Step 3: Execute the query to create the work table   DatabaseConnector::executeSql(connection, translated_drug_sql)      # Step 4: Query the created table to retrieve results   # Use SqlRender and translate for the SELECT query as well   select_query <- "SELECT * FROM @workDatabaseSchema.@workTableName"      rendered_select <- SqlRender::render(     select_query,     workDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$workDatabaseSchema,     workTableName = workTableName   )      translated_select <- SqlRender::translate(     rendered_select,     targetDialect = executionSettings$getDbms(),     tempEmulationSchema = executionSettings$tempEmulationSchema   )      results <- DatabaseConnector::querySql(connection, translated_select)      # Disconnect from the database   executionSettings$disconnect()      cli::cli_alert_success("Drug exposure summary retrieved successfully")      return(results) } # In your task file (e.g., 02_drug_exposure_analysis.R) source(here::here("analysis/src/execute_sql_queries.R"))  # Execute the SQL query and collect results drug_summary <- get_drug_exposure_summary(   executionSettings = executionSettings,   workTableName = "drug_exp_summary",   studyStartDate = "2020-01-01",   studyEndDate = "2023-12-31" )  # Now work with results in R head(drug_summary)

Picard provides saveWork() make Git commits easy new users: saveWork() convenience wrapper around Git operations handles common workflow : Show changed - Displays modified, new, deleted files Confirm commit - Asks approve changes proceeding Stage files - Automatically stages changes Commit - Creates commit message Pull remote - Gets latest changes team Push remote - Syncs commit shared repository Key features: Protection mistakes: Prevents accidentally committing main branch Interactive confirmation: Shows exactly files changed asks confirm committing Work--progress commits: Use push = FALSE commit locally without pushing (useful saving work mid-session): Automatic branch creation: feature branch doesn’t exist, saveWork() creates Syncs team: Pulls remote changes pushing work, reducing merge conflicts Full function signature: new Git users: Use saveWork() regularly develop. simplifies entire commit workflow protects common Git mistakes. advanced users: can use Git directly prefer. Many developers commit changes go using: Either approach works—use ’s comfortable workflow.

title: Evidence Generation Plan
text: Evidence Generation Plan (EGP) required document Picard pipeline. structured specification document bridges research questions analysis code. EGP defines analytical experiment layman’s terms drives pipeline—code implements specifications document . serves three critical purposes: Precision: Translates research questions exact analytical specifications (numerators, denominators, observation windows, inclusion/exclusion logic) Transparency: Makes choices explicit justified, enabling others understand validate approach Implementation: Provides sufficient detail programmers can implement vision without ambiguity Key insight: EGPs one-size-fits-templates. adapt fundamentally based study design: Surveillance studies (prevalence, incidence) emphasize observation periods, lookback windows, numerator/denominator specifications (example: Type 2 Diabetes prevalence) Characterization studies describe patient populations, baseline features, treatment patterns, temporal trends (example: Psoriasis patients) Outcome studies focus exposure definitions, time--risk, outcome specifications Treatment pathway studies detail era logic, gap collapsing rules, sequence analysis Note EGP file automatically generated launch study. template contains comments guidance help fill details. vignette teaches principles EGP construction shows examples different study types. ’ll adapt structure match research questions.

title: Evidence Generation Plan
text: Define study populations important cohorts precisely. cohort definitions three main components: Entry criteria: patients enter cohort? Inclusion criteria: additional criteria must met stay cohort? Exit criteria: patients exit cohort (.e censored death end continuous observation)? examples cohort definitions can structured based study design: Psoriasis Example: Type 2 Diabetes Example: guiding principles defining cohort definitions EGP: index date clearly defined. Typically index date sequence events second occurrence (avoid immortal time bias). index date occurrence clinical event (diagnosis, procedure, medication dispensing) rather arbitrary date (calendar date, visit). Always provide temporal logic sequence events. example prior treatment state either time window relative index date explicit whether limit first-ever occurrence allow multiple occurrences per patient Clearly define exit criteria (death, end observation) ensure proper censoring follow-analyses Programming tip: Cohort definitions Atlas come markdown format pasted EGP can clean format.
code: ### Newly-Diagnosed Psoriasis Cohort  #### Cohort Entry - First visit date with Psoriasis diagnosis code - Requirement: >= 2 Psoriasis diagnosis visits on different dates, 30-365 days apart - Requirement: 365 days continuous prior observation - Limit occurrence to the initial event per patient (first-ever diagnosis)  #### Inclusion Criteria - NO prior psoriasis-related treatments in 365 days prior to index  #### Cohort Exit - End of continuous observation, OR - Death ### Type 2 Diabetes Cohort  #### Cohort Entry - 2 codes of Type 2 Diabetes on different dates, at least 30 days apart - the second code is the index date - Limit occurrence to the initial event per patient (first-ever diagnosis)  #### Inclusion Criteria - NO occurrence of Type 1 Diabetes diagnosis code at any time prior to index date - NO occurrence of Gestational Diabetes diagnosis code at any time prior to index date  #### Cohort Exit - End of continuous observation, OR - Death

title: Evidence Generation Plan
text: section varies greatly study type. keyprinciple: specific enough programmer can implement without guessing. Want convey experiment logic way easy codify experiment set parameters. examples specify analyses different study types. Provide much detail possible analysis. Prevalence Example: Characterization Example:
code: ### Prevalence Calculation: Any-Time Point Estimate  **Period of Interest:** Each calendar year 2015-2024  **Observation Requirements:** - Include: Anyone with >= 1 day enrollment in the year - Minimum continuous: None (any fragmented enrollment allowed)  **Lookback:** - Search 365 days prior to period for any Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis  **Numerator:** - Patients with Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis code prior to OR during period  **Denominator:** - All patients with any enrollment during period of interest  **Calculation:** Prevalence = (Numerator / Denominator) per 100,000 ### Baseline Characteristics  **Baseline Window:** 365 days prior to index date through 1 day prior  **Demographics Reported:** - Age at index date - Sex, Race, Ethnicity - Payer type - Calendar year of index  **Comorbidities:** - Report any diagnosis code during baseline window for:   - Autoimmune/Inflammatory conditions (Rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn's disease, etc.)   - Cardiovascular disease   - Metabolic disease (Diabetes, Obesity)   - [Organized by clinical system]  **Treatments:** - Any dispensing during baseline window for:   - Topical agents (corticosteroids, calcineurin inhibitors, retinoids)   - Systemic agents (methotrexate, acitretin, cyclosporine, apremilast)   - Biologic agents (TNF inhibitors, IL-17i, IL-23i, JAK inhibitors)   - Other (phototherapy, oral steroids)  ### Treatment Pathways During Follow-up  **Gap Collapsing Rules:** - Topical agents: Collapse gaps of <30 days - Systemic agents: Collapse gaps of <30-60 days depending on agent - Biologic agents: Collapse gaps of <60-120 days depending on specific drug  **Combination Logic:** - Different drug classes overlapping >= 60 days = counted as combination therapy  **Episode Minimum:** - Episodes of <14 days are excluded (too short to assess effectiveness)  **Output:** - Sunburst plot showing first 4 treatment steps in newly-diagnosed population - Table: Median days on treatment by therapy class and line number

previous_headings: Why Git and renv Matter for Picard Studies
title: Launching a Picard Study
text: Git tracks every change code documentation throughout project lifecycle. pipeline-driven studies, provides critical benefits: Code reproducibility: Git records exactly version code produced results. essential regulatory compliance peer review. Audit trail: Every commit includes made change, , . accountability crucial study documentation QC. Collaboration: Multiple team members can work different analysis tasks simultaneously without conflicts. Git helps merge changes cleanly. Pipeline provenance: pipeline generates results, can trace results back exact code commit produced . Disaster recovery: Git acts backup. something goes wrong, can revert previous working state. Feature branches: can test new analysis approaches isolated branches merging production. Picard’s Branching Model: Picard enforces strict branching workflow makes Git requirement, optional: Main branch: Protected branch used release-ready code. Production pipelines executed release branches created main. Develop branch: Integration branch team members merge tested features. testing QC happens code ready production. Feature/task branches: Individual developers work analysis tasks isolated branches, submit pull requests review merging develop. branching strategy ensures: - Production work never runs unstable code main - changes reviewed reaching production - Testing happens controlled environment deployment - Team members can work independently without interfering production results Without Git disciplined branching approach, safe way run production pipelines study multiple contributors. studies data security reproducibility paramount, Git optional—’s foundational. Note: Git automatically initialized create repository. specify gitRemote setup, need manually connect local repository remote push changes. See Setting Git Version Control section instructions.

previous_headings: Why Git and renv Matter for Picard Studies
title: Launching a Picard Study
text: R packages constantly updated. Different versions can produce different results, even identical data code. renv solves creating snapshot R environment: Reproducibility across time: renv.lock captures exact package versions used analysis. Six months six years later, can restore identical environment reproduce every result. Team consistency: collaborative studies, different team members might different package versions installed. renv ensures everyone uses versions, eliminating “works machine” problems. Dependency management: renv tracks just direct dependencies nested dependencies. package depends package B version 1.2, renv captures relationship. Production safety: promoting analysis code production, renv ensures dependencies compatible tested together. Regulatory compliance: studies subject validation requirements, renv provides documented evidence package versions captured reproducible. collaborative, regulated research environments, renv essential ensuring results truly reproducible team member point future. Note: Unlike Git, renv automatically initialized repository. must set renv . highly encouraged : - Ensures analysis produces consistent results run team members - Protects package updates silently break pipeline - Creates audit trail package versions used study See Setting renv Reproducibility section get started.

text: pattern uses inputs/conceptSets/R/import_capr_concept_set.R requires Capr package. Capr provides R interface building OMOP concept sets programmatically: See Capr documentation detailed syntax complex concept set definitions.
code: library(Capr)  conceptSetManifest <- loadConceptSetManifest()  # Example 1: Diabetes mellitus concepts diabetesConcepts <- cs(   descendants(201820),  # Type 2 diabetes mellitus   descendants(443238)   # Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus ) conceptSetManifest$addCaprConceptSet(   conceptSetName = "Diabetes",   conceptSet = diabetesConcepts )  # Example 2: Antidiabetic drugs antidiabeticDrugs <- cs(   descendants(21600960),  # Metformin   descendants(21601389),  # Sulfonylureas   descendants(21602722),  # SGLT2 inhibitors   sourceCode = TRUE ) conceptSetManifest$addCaprConceptSet(   conceptSetName = "AntidiabeticDrugs",   conceptSet = antidiabeticDrugs )

title: Loading Inputs: Getting Started
text: pattern uses inputs/cohorts/R/import_capr_cohort.R requires Capr package. Capr provides fluent interface building cohort definitions R: See Capr documentation detailed examples.
code: library(Capr)  cohortManifest <- loadCohortManifest()  # Example: Type 2 Diabetes cohort with washout period t2dCohort <- cohort(   entry = entry(     condition(       descendants(201820),  # Type 2 diabetes       on = "conditionStart"     ) %>%       filter(         relationshipDomain(           "measurement",           descendants(3002962)  # HbA1c measurement         ),         duringInterval(daysBefore = 365, daysAfter = 1)       )   ),   attrition(     "No prior T2D",     !condition(descendants(201820), on = "conditionStart") %>%       during(daysBefore = 365)   ) )  cohortManifest$addCaprCohort(   cohortName = "Type2Diabetes_HbA1c",   cohort = t2dCohort )

title: Loading Inputs: Getting Started
text: pattern uses inputs/cohorts/R/import_sql_cohort.R. Custom SQL cohorts let define cohorts using hand-written SQL queries. Place SQL files inputs/cohorts/sql/: SQL files must follow SqlRender conventions parameters prefixed @: Key SqlRender parameters: - @target_cohort_id — numeric ID cohort - @target_database_schema — schema results written - @cdm_database_schema — CDM database schema location - @vocabulary_database_schema — vocabulary schema location Always use DELETE INSERT make cohort idempotent (can re-run without duplication).
code: cohortManifest <- loadCohortManifest()  # Add a custom SQL cohort cohortManifest$addSqlCohort(   cohortName = "MyCustomCohort",   sqlPath = here::here("inputs/cohorts/sql/my_custom_cohort.sql"),   # SqlRender parameters (will substitute @param in the SQL file)   targetCohortId = 1001,   cdmDatabaseSchema = "cdm" ) -- Cohort: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes DELETE FROM @target_database_schema.cohort WHERE cohort_definition_id = @target_cohort_id;  INSERT INTO @target_database_schema.cohort   (cohort_definition_id, subject_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date) SELECT   @target_cohort_id as cohort_definition_id,   person_id as subject_id,   condition_start_date as cohort_start_date,   DATEADD(day, 365, condition_start_date) as cohort_end_date FROM @cdm_database_schema.condition_occurrence WHERE condition_concept_id IN (201820, 443238)   AND condition_start_date >= '2015-01-01';

title: Loading Inputs: Getting Started
text: pattern uses inputs/cohorts/R/build_dependent_cohorts.R. Derived cohorts relationships existing base cohorts. base cohorts must imported first (via ATLAS, Capr, SQL). Relationship types: - Temporal — Base cohort another cohort /- Union — Combine multiple cohorts - Complement — Base cohort excluding another cohort - O-Prior-T — Outcome treatment starts - T-Prior-O — Treatment outcome - Censor — Cohort censoring date See Manifest: Architecture, Workflows, Helpers comprehensive examples derived cohort types.
code: cohortManifest <- loadCohortManifest()  # Ensure base cohorts exist # - CohortId 1: Chronic Kidney Disease # - CohortId 2: Type 2 Diabetes  # Example 1: Temporal - CKD in patients with prior T2D cohortManifest$buildSubsetCohortTemporal(   baseCohortId = 1,   subsetParent = 2,   temporalType = "prior",   daysBefore = 365,   daysAfter = 0,   newCohortName = "CKD_With_Prior_T2D" )  # Example 2: Union - CKD or T2D patients cohortManifest$buildUnionCohort(   cohortIds = c(1, 2),   newCohortName = "CKD_or_T2D" )  # Example 3: Complement - CKD without T2D cohortManifest$buildComplementCohort(   baseCohortId = 1,   excludeCohortId = 2,   newCohortName = "CKD_Without_T2D" )  # Example 4: O-Prior-T - Outcome prior to treatment # (Outcome cohort excludes those with outcome before/during treatment) cohortManifest$buildOPriorTCohort(   outcomeCohortId = 3,   treatmentCohortId = 2,   daysBefore = 30,   newCohortName = "Outcome_Prior_to_Treatment" )

title: The Manifest: Architecture, Workflows, and Helpers
text: Filters outcome cohort based whether prior target (exposure) event exists. Optionally restricts lookback time window.

title: The Manifest: Architecture, Workflows, and Helpers
text: Rebuilding derived pipeline new parameters → "derived". base cohort registrations ATLAS imports preserved; just re-run $build*() calls. Corrupt restructured database → "manifest". Source files kept; call initCohortManifest() re-register via $add*() $importAtlasCohorts(). Complete restart → "full". Requires executionSettings drop OMOP tables. Use caution. scopes prompt confirmation. Pass confirm = FALSE skip scripts. archive = TRUE, SQLite database backed .archive/ timestamp deleted, allowing recovery needed.
code: # Rebuild derived pipeline only (keeps base cohorts) resetCohortManifest(manifest = manifest, scope = "derived")  # Wipe manifest DB, keep json/ and sql/ source files # With archive: creates timestamped backup before deletion resetCohortManifest(cohortsFolderPath = here::here("inputs/cohorts"),                     scope = "manifest",                     archive = TRUE)  # create backup at inputs/cohorts/.archive/  # Full nuclear reset (also drops OMOP cohort tables) resetCohortManifest(manifest          = manifest,                     scope             = "full",                     executionSettings = execSettings,                     archive           = TRUE)

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Picard built Ulysses framework, follows philosophy real-world evidence (RWE) studies organized versioned software projects. Just databases require schema organize data, RWE studies benefit standard directory structure organize code, inputs, analyses, outputs. standardization enables: Reproducibility: Anyone can understand project structure glance Collaboration: Multiple contributors follow conventions Automation: Consistent organization enables reliable workflows tooling Version control: Clear separation concerns makes git history meaningful Picard uses Ulysses repository structure, adding specialized directories configuration cohort-based studies, Evidence Generation Plans, results dissemination. vignette describes standard Ulysses repository structure created initialize project using launchUlyssesRepo().

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Ulysses repository organizes folders match flow real-world evidence study: Workflow sequence: 1. Initialize metadata (config.yml defines databases credentials) 2. Load create inputs (cohorts concept sets) 3. Execute analysis tasks (code analysis/tasks runs using inputs) 4. Raw results written exec/results organized database version 5. Post-processing (migrations) formatting (excel, CSV) 6. Dissemination via Study Hub (quarto website) formatted exports
code: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ 1. META & CONFIG                                                │ │    (config.yml, README.md, NEWS.md, main.R, test_main.R)       │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 2. INPUTS                                                       │ │    inputs/cohorts/ + inputs/conceptSets/                      │ │    (Define phenotypes, cohorts, covariates)                    │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 3. ANALYSIS                                                     │ │    analysis/tasks/ + analysis/src/ + analysis/migrations/     │ │    (Execute analyses, generate statistics)                      │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 4. EXECUTION OUTPUT                                             │ │    exec/results/[database]/[version]/[task]/                  │ │    (Raw results by task, database, version)                     │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 5. DISSEMINATION                                                │ │    dissemination/export/ + dissemination/quarto/               │ │    (Format results, create Study Hub website)                   │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Central configuration file specifying parameters needed establish database connections. Uses YAML format two section types: default: Universal study settings (project name, version) block headers: Database-specific configurations (dbms, credentials, schemas) source block header task file, pipeline runs using block’s configuration, enabling multi-database studies. Important: Connection details vary database system. codebase distinguishes : Snowflake: Uses connectionString format (JDBC connection string) PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, Redshift: Use server port fields Protecting Credentials !expr: !expr tag (config package) allows evaluate R code within config file. critical security: enables pulling credentials environment variables rather storing plain text config.yml. can use R function wrapped !expr retrieve credentials, including: Environment variables: !expr Sys.getenv('VAR_NAME') Keyring package: !expr keyring::key_get(service = 'picard', username = 'atlasUser') Custom functions: !expr my_secure_fetch_credential('db_password') Best practice: Always use !expr secure credential storage system. Never store passwords connection strings plain text config.yml commit git. Common credentials: dbms: Database type (snowflake, sql server, postgresql, mysql, oracle, redshift) user: Database username (environment variable via !expr Sys.getenv()) password: Database password (environment variable) databaseName: Internal reference name (snake_case database + snapshot date) databaseLabel: Pretty name output formatting cdmDatabaseSchema: Schema containing OMOP CDM tables (format: schema database.schema) vocabDatabaseSchema: Schema containing vocabulary tables (usually cdmDatabaseSchema) workDatabaseSchema: Schema user write access (cohort tables intermediary work) tempEmulationSchema: Optional schema temp tables (snowflake, oracle) cohortTable: Name cohort table create (default: {repoName}_{databaseName}) Example config.yml Snowflake PostgreSQL: Setting environment variables: .Renviron file (project home directory): Load running pipeline: readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
code: user: !expr Sys.getenv('dbUser')        # Evaluates R code: retrieves DB_USER from environment password: !expr Sys.getenv('dbPassword') # Evaluates R code: retrieves DB_PASSWORD from environment # Config File for my_study  default:   projectName: my_study   version: 1.0.0  # Snowflake: Uses connectionString format snowflake_prod:   dbms: snowflake   connectionString: !expr Sys.getenv('dbConnectionString')   user: !expr Sys.getenv('dbUser')   password: !expr Sys.getenv('dbPassword')   databaseName: snowflake_prod_20260101   databaseLabel: Snowflake Production   cdmDatabaseSchema: omop_schema   vocabDatabaseSchema: omop_schema   workDatabaseSchema: work_schema   tempEmulationSchema: temp_schema   cohortTable: my_study_cohorts  # PostgreSQL: Uses server/port format postgres_local:   dbms: postgresql   server: localhost   port: 5432   user: !expr Sys.getenv('pgUser')   password: !expr Sys.getenv('pgPassword')   databaseName: postgres_local_20260101   databaseLabel: PostgreSQL Local   cdmDatabaseSchema: public   vocabDatabaseSchema: public   workDatabaseSchema: results   cohortTable: my_study_pg_cohorts dbUser=your_db_username dbPassword=your_db_password dbConnectionString=jdbc:snowflake://account.snowflakecomputing.com:443 pgUser=postgres_user pgPassword=postgres_password

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Individual R scripts perform analytical steps. task self-contained unit : Loads necessary inputs (cohorts, concept sets, configuration) Performs specific analytical step Saves results standardized output location Note: Cohort generation built-Picard feature handled automatically run pipeline. create cohort generation task file. Tasks start cohorts generated. See Running Pipeline details pipeline execution cohort generation. Tasks named sequentially (01_, 02_, etc.) executed order: task independent can tested individually development using testStudyTask(). Task File Format Validation: Picard enforces standardized task file structure ensure consistency enable add-modules import external task files. Use makeTaskFile() create new tasks—automatically generates files correct format. Required task file sections (validated validateStudyTask()): . Meta: Metadata task (title, author, description, purpose) B. Dependencies: Input files objects required task C. Connection Settings: Configuration block name pipeline version (uses template variables !||configBlock||! !||pipelineVersion||!) Create executionSettings <- createExecutionSettingsFromConfig(configBlock = configBlock) Create outputFolder <- setOutputFolder(executionSettings = executionSettings, pipelineVersion = pipelineVersion, taskName = "task_name") E. Script: Actual analysis code performs task Validation Rules: task files must pass validation execution: File must exist readable five sections (, B, C, D, E) must present Template variables !||configBlock||! !||pipelineVersion||! must defined section C Section D must create executionSettings object via createExecutionSettingsFromConfig() Section D must create outputFolder object via setOutputFolder() Section E must contain actual code (just comments template placeholders) Validation Matters: Standardized task files enable: Consistency: tasks follow structure across study Portability: Add-modules packages can import external tasks conform format Reproducibility: Clear documentation dependencies configuration Automation: pipeline can reliably execute tasks knowing meet structural requirements Example: task follows required format, import another Picard study add-package rather rewriting .

text: Post-processing scripts clean reshape pipeline results dissemination. Migrations numbered correspond source analysis tasks (e.g., 02_migrate_surveillance.R cleans output task 02_surveillance.R). Purpose: orchestratePipelineExport() binds raw results metadata, migrations handle data wrangling tasks weren’t necessary execution: Aggregating: Combine results across subgroups time periods Standardizing: Apply demographic weighting statistical adjustments Pivoting: Reshape long format wide publication tables Deriving: Calculate new metrics (confidence intervals, effect size categories, standardized rates) Filtering: Remove rows minimum cell counts meeting exclusion criteria Workflow: Example Structure: migration reads dissemination/export/merge/ (raw exported results), performs transformations, writes cleaned data dissemination/export/pretty/ final dissemination.
code: Task 02: Pipeline Execution     ↓ orchestratePipelineExport() → raw results to dissemination/export/merge/     ↓ Migration 02: Data Wrangling (02_migrate_surveillance.R)     ↓ dissemination/export/pretty/ → finalized, publication-ready results analysis/migrations/ ├── 02_migrate_surveillance.R │   # Aggregate surveillance counts by age/sex strata │   # Apply census weighting for standardization │   # Generate crude and standardized prevalence/incidence rates ├── 03_migrate_comparative.R │   # Pivot comparative analysis results wide │   # Calculate confidence intervals and p-values └── 05_migrate_sensitivity.R     # Combine sensitivity analysis variants into summary table

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Cohort definitions defining study populations. cohort set persons satisfy one inclusion criteria duration time. OHDSI research, cohorts foundation observational studies. Cohort Definition Types: Uses CIRCE-Java library standardized representation Stored JSON files json/ folder Ensures consistent serialization SQL: definition always generates identical populations Typically imported ATLAS Manual SQL queries sql/ folder Used CIRCE express logic need Subsets existing cohorts (apply additional inclusion criteria) Unions multiple cohorts (combine populations) Stored sql/ folder derivative definitions Folder Structure: cohortsLoad.csv: CSV file metadata cohort. loadCohortManifest() called, file used enrich CohortDef objects matching file_name actual cohort files. Used track cohorts came organize tags. Columns: - atlasId: ATLAS cohort ID (integer, e.g., 1, 42) - label: Display name (character, e.g., "Type 2 Diabetes patients") - category: Broad grouping (character, e.g., "Disease Populations") - subCategory: Sub-grouping (character, optional, e.g., "Endocrine") - file_name: Relative path JSON file (character, e.g., "json/t2dm_patients.json") Example: loaded, metadata fields converted tags cohort later querying (e.g., $queryCohortsByTag(), $queryCohortsByLabel()). cohortManifest.sqlite: SQLite database created/managed CohortManifest class. Contains cohort_manifest table tracking cohort metadata: Workflow: Create cohortsLoad.csv metadata cohorts (use createBlankCohortsLoadFile()) Import ATLAS cohort JSON definitions json/ folder (use importAtlasCohorts()) Add custom SQL dependency-based cohorts sql/ (e.g., subsets, unions) Call loadCohortManifest() scan directories enrich cohortsLoad.csv metadata First load creates cohortManifest.sqlite database; subsequent loads verify file hashes Use CohortManifest methods query cohorts: $queryCohortById(1), $queryCohortsByTag("category: Disease Populations") See also: Loading Inputs detailed guidance creating, importing, managing cohort manifests.
code: inputs/cohorts/ ├── json/ │   ├── 001_primaryPopulation.json     # CIRCE-based from ATLAS │   ├── 002_comparativeArm.json        # CIRCE-based from ATLAS │   └── 003_outcomeDefinition.json     # CIRCE-based from ATLAS ├── sql/ │   ├── 004_primarySubset.sql          # Subset of cohort 001 │   └── 005_combinedPopulation.sql     # Union of cohorts 001 & 002 ├── cohortsLoad.csv                    # Metadata index for cohort enrichment └── cohortManifest.sqlite              # Provenance & metadata tracking database atlasId,label,category,subCategory,file_name 1,Type 2 Diabetes,Disease Populations,Endocrine,json/t2dm_patients.json 2,Diabetes Complications,Disease Populations,Endocrine,json/diabetes_complications.json

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Stores CIRCE-based concept set definitions identifying cohorts extracting covariates: conceptSetsLoad.csv: Similar cohortsLoad.csv, CSV provides metadata enriching ConceptSetDef objects. Columns: - atlasId: ATLAS concept set ID (integer, e.g., 456, 789) - label: Display name (character, e.g., "Antidiabetic medications") - category: Broad grouping (character, e.g., "Medications" "Diagnoses") - subCategory: Optional sub-grouping (character, e.g., "Endocrine Drugs") - domain: OMOP clinical domain (required, character): - drug_exposure - Medication/drug concept sets - condition_occurrence - Diagnosis concept sets - measurement - Lab/test result concept sets - procedure - Medical procedure concept sets - observation - Observation concept sets - device_exposure - Device/equipment concept sets - visit_occurrence - Visit type concept sets - init - yet classified (placeholder) - sourceCode: Rarely used; TRUE concept set represents source codes instead standard concepts (character: TRUE FALSE) - file_name: Relative path JSON file (character, e.g., "json/hypertension.json") Example: loaded, metadata fields converted tags concept set querying (e.g., $getConceptSetsByTag(), $getConceptSetsByLabel()). conceptSetManifest.sqlite: SQLite database created/managed ConceptSetManifest class. Contains concept_set_manifest table: Workflow: Create update conceptSetsLoad.csv metadata (use createBlankConceptSetsLoadFile()) Import ATLAS concept set JSON definitions json/ folder (use importAtlasConceptSets()) Call loadConceptSetManifest() scan directories enrich conceptSetsLoad.csv metadata First load creates conceptSetManifest.sqlite database; subsequent loads verify file hashes Use ConceptSetManifest methods query: $getConceptSetById(456), $getConceptSetsByTag("domain: drug_exposure") Extract source codes dependencies using $extractSourceCodes() (requires ExecutionSettings) See also: Loading Inputs detailed guidance creating, importing, managing concept set manifests.

text: Results exported pipeline via orchestratePipelineExport() processed various formats dissemination: Workflow: Merge phase: orchestratePipelineExport(pipelineVersion, dbIds) reads raw results exec/results/ across databases tasks given version. combines results long-format CSV files dissemination/export/merge/, along reference files (cohortKey, databaseInfo, schema_review). Format phase: Migration scripts (e.g., 02_migrate_surveillance.R) read dissemination/export/merge/ perform data wrangling, reshaping, formatting. Output depends use case: publication/reports: Write formatted Excel/CSV files dissemination/export/pretty/ Study Hub: Write data tables figures dissemination/export/studyHubOutput/ Dissemination: Files pretty/ copied directly publications referenced static Quarto reports Files studyHubOutput/ sourced dynamically Quarto files dissemination/quarto/ create interactive tables figures Study Hub website
code: dissemination/export/ ├── merge/                 # Raw merged results from orchestratePipelineExport() │   ├── cohortKey.csv      # Cohort definitions (generated) │   ├── databaseInfo.csv   # Database metadata (generated) │   ├── schema_review.csv  # Schema validation (generated) │   ├── task_01_results.csv │   ├── task_02_results.csv │   └── task_03_results.csv ├── pretty/                # Formatted results from migration scripts (Excel, CSV, etc.) │   ├── mainResults.xlsx │   ├── sensitivity_analyses.xlsx │   └── suppTable1_demographics.xlsx └── studyHubOutput/        # Files sourced by the Study Hub for dynamic rendering     ├── table1_demographics.csv     ├── figure1_incidence.csv     └── results_summary.json

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: folder contains execution artifacts logs running pipeline. Results organized database, pipeline version, task support parallel development multi-database studies.

previous_headings: Elements of the Ulysses Repository > Exec Folder
title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Raw results pipeline execution, organized hierarchically: results organized: Database folders: One folder per database configured config.yml. Database folder name snake_case version databaseName config (e.g., optum_dod → optum_dod/) Version folders: Within database, results organized pipelineVersion (e.g., 1.0.0/, 1.0.1/). special dev/ folder holds temporary test results Task folders: Within version, one folder per task created setOutputFolder() (e.g., 00_buildCohorts/, 01_descriptiveStats/) Task file execution: task script analysis/tasks/ creates output folder via: creates: exec/results/{databaseName}/{pipelineVersion}/{taskName}/ task results (CSVs, logs, etc.) written. Development vs. Production: - Development mode (test_main.R): Uses pipelineVersion = "dev" results go exec/results/{db}/dev/. Results temporary don’t interfere versioned runs. - Production mode (main.R): Uses semantic versioning (e.g., pipelineVersion = "1.0.0") results go exec/results/{db}/1.0.0/. Results retained post-processing archival.
code: exec/results/ ├── primary_db/                         # Created per config.yml database ID │   ├── 1.0.0/                          # Versioned results (semantic versions) │   │   ├── 00_buildCohorts/ │   │   │   ├── cohortCounts.csv │   │   │   ├── cohortResults.csv │   │   │   └── buildLog.txt │   │   ├── 01_descriptiveStats/ │   │   │   ├── demographics.csv │   │   │   └── flowchart.csv │   │   ├── 02_surveillance/ │   │   │   └── incidenceCounts.csv │   │   └── 03_primaryAnalysis/ │   │       └── modelResults.csv │   ├── 1.1.0/                          # Another versioned run │   │   ├── 00_buildCohorts/ │   │   └── ... │   └── dev/                            # Development/test results (temporary) │       ├── 00_buildCohorts/ │       └── ... └── secondary_db/     ├── 1.0.0/     └── dev/ outputFolder <- setOutputFolder(   executionSettings = executionSettings,   pipelineVersion = pipelineVersion,   taskName = "00_buildCohorts" )

title: Post-Processing and Dissemination
text: pipeline completes via execStudyPipeline(), raw analytical outputs stored exec/results/[database]/[version]/ organized task. post-processing phase merges results across databases prepares dissemination. Post-processing occurs three stages: Merge & Export — Combine results across databases via runPostProcessing() Schema Review — Inspect data types structure Quality Control — Validate cohort completeness generate metadata Results saved dissemination/export/merge/v{version}/ reference files, QC reports, optional dissemination scripts final formatting.

title: Post-Processing and Dissemination
text: production pipeline completes, call runPostProcessing() orchestrate merge: function: Auto-discovers tasks specified version across databases Merges results across databases task via importAndBind() Adds databaseId column results track data source Creates reference files: cohortManifestSnapshot.csv, databaseInfo.csv Reviews file schemas reviewExportSchema() Validates cohorts validateCohortResults() Generates execution metadata QC status Supports test mode (non-fatal QC checks) development versions
code: library(picard)  # Merge results for version 1.0.0 across databases runPostProcessing(   pipelineVersion = "1.0.0",   dbIds = c("database_1", "database_2"),   resultsPath = here::here("exec/results"),   exportPath = here::here("dissemination/export/merge"),   cohortsFolderPath = here::here("inputs/cohorts"),   testMode = FALSE )

title: Post-Processing and Dissemination
text: Pass testMode = TRUE development versions (e.g., “dev”, “test”) skip non-fatal QC checks: default, test mode auto-enabled non-semver version strings.
code: runPostProcessing(   pipelineVersion = "dev",   dbIds = c("database_1"),   testMode = TRUE  # Non-fatal QC checks )

title: Post-Processing and Dissemination
text: processing, results organized dissemination/export/merge/v{version}/:
code: v1.0.0/ ├── cohort_counts.csv              # Cohort counts merged across databases ├── characterization.csv           # Task results merged across databases ├── primary_analysis.csv           # Task results merged across databases ├── cohortManifestSnapshot.csv     # Reference: cohort metadata at execution ├── databaseInfo.csv               # Reference: database details ├── schema_review.csv              # Schema inspection results ├── qc_cohortValidation.csv        # QC: cohort completeness validation └── qc_processMeta.csv             # QC: execution metadata

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: official execution script main.R project root. : Validates code state (git clean, changes committed) Increments study version (semantic versioning) Runs complete pipeline validations Creates release branch reproducibility Generates PR metadata code review Saves production-quality results versioned folder

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: Run main.R : Formal analysis runs: Official results publications regulatory submissions Final results: ’re confident code ready version history Multi-database comparisons: Ensures consistency across databases Code review: Results go PR review acceptance Production mode places versioned results exec/results/[database]/[version]/ (e.g., 1.0.0/).

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: running production mode: Commit changes: git add . git commit -m "..." develop branch (feature branch): git checkout develop Pull latest changes: git pull Verify configuration: Check config.yml correctness Delete unused builders; keep ones need See Loading Inputs detailed guidance builder type can also using saveWork() function describe Developing Pipeline save work prepare production.

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
code: # Navigate to study repository setwd("~/studies/myStudy")  # Run production pipeline with patch version increment source("main.R")  # When prompted, answer questions about version increment: # What type of version change? [major/minor/patch] # You typically choose: "patch" (bug fixes), "minor" (new analyses), "major" (breaking changes)

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
code: library(picard)  # Run production pipeline directly execStudyPipeline(   configBlock = c("primaryDB", "secondaryDB"),   updateType = "minor"  # Version increment type )

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: Choose appropriate semantic version increment: PATCH (1.0.0 → 1.0.1): Bug fixes, data corrections, new analyses MINOR (1.0.0 → 1.1.0): New analyses features added (backward compatible) MAJOR (1.0.0 → 2.0.0): Breaking changes study redesign

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: Production execution follows five main phases: Pre-Pipeline: Auto-discover source builder scripts inputs/conceptSets/R/ inputs/cohorts/R/ Concept set builders run first (importAtlas, importCapr, custom) Cohort builders run second (importAtlas, importCapr, importSql, buildDependentCohorts) Manifests loaded populated definitions Setup: Validate configuration, load execution settings, create output directories Generate Cohorts: Instantiate cohort definitions database, validate cohort counts Run Analysis Tasks: task analysis/tasks/, load configuration, execute task code, check errors, record results Post-Processing: Generate version logs, create PR metadata, save PENDING_PR.md

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: Production mode validates code state strictly. Common issues: “run production pipeline main branch!” - Solution: Switch develop: git checkout develop “Code state validation failed - uncommitted changes” - Solution: Commit changes: git add . git commit -m "..." “Cohort manifest found” - Solution: See Loading Inputs

title: Running the Pipeline: Production Execution
text: Production mode enables structured code review: Run pipeline: source("main.R") develop branch Review PENDING_PR.md: Check proposed version, changes logged NEWS.md Review code: Inspect changes release branch: git checkout release/1.1.0 Create PR: Use details PENDING_PR.md create PR GitHub/Bitbucket Merge: review approval, merge main Cleanup: Run clearPendingPR() remove metadata file

title: CohortManifest R6 Class — CohortManifest
text: R6 class manages collection CohortDef objects maintains metadata SQLite database. CohortManifest class manages multiple cohort definitions stores metadata SQLite database located inputs/cohorts/cohortManifest.sqlite. CohortDef assigned sequential ID based position manifest. detection phase ATLAS maintenance workflow. Use identify ATLAS cohorts changed, optionally call updateAtlasCohorts() apply updates. Changes detected comparing expression JSON hashes. method updates ATLAS cohorts changed remote repository. : Fetches current definitions ATLAS Updates JSON files disk Recomputes stores hashes Updates manifest database Use checkAtlasChanges() first identify cohorts changed, call method apply updates. Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() workDatabaseSchema target schema cohortTable desired table name tempEmulationSchema needed database platform Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() workDatabaseSchema target schema cohortTable desired table name Requires executionSettings set valid database connection, workDatabaseSchema, cohortTable. Execution flow: Build dependency graph CohortDef objects Validate circular dependencies (error found) Topologically sort cohorts dependencies (parents children) cohort topological order: circe cohorts: check SQL hash (existing logic) dependent cohorts: compute dependency hash parent hashes + rule Render execute SQL (circe uses SqlRender parameters, dependent uses metadata JSON) Record checksums dependency hashes database Report results cohort_type, depends_on, dependency_status columns Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() cdmDatabaseSchema (OMOP CDM data resides) workDatabaseSchema (cohort results written) cohortTable (destination table name) tempEmulationSchema needed database platform

title: ConceptSetManifest R6 Class — ConceptSetManifest
text: R6 class manages collection ConceptSetDef objects maintains metadata SQLite database. ConceptSetManifest class manages multiple concept set definitions stores metadata SQLite database located inputs/conceptSets/conceptSetManifest.sqlite. ConceptSetDef assigned sequential ID based position manifest. Processing Steps: Validates concept set IDs exist active Loads concept set JSON Capr object using Capr::readConceptSet() Combines using set logic (private helper combine_capr_concept_sets()) Exports combined Capr object JSON json/ directory Registers new combined concept set manifest Returns new concept set ID Concept Set Combination Logic: Includes: included concepts across sets Descendants: concepts marked descendants Excludes: excluded concepts (without descendants) Exclude+Descendants: concepts exclude descendants Requirements: Capr package must installed source concept sets must active valid JSON files detection phase ATLAS maintenance workflow. Use identify ATLAS concept sets changed, optionally call updateAtlasConceptSets() apply updates. Changes detected comparing expression JSON hashes. method updates ATLAS concept sets changed remote repository. : Calls checkAtlasConceptSets() identify changes changed concept set: fetches current definition, updates JSON file, updates hash manifest Refreshes -memory manifest Use checkAtlasConceptSets() first identify concept sets changed, call method apply updates. Requirements: ExecutionSettings must initialized valid database connection ExecutionSettings must cdmDatabaseSchema optionally tempEmulationSchema set User must READ access OMOP concept concept_ancestor tables Processing: Retrieves concept set definition (CIRCE JSON) ID Builds SQL query using CirceR::buildConceptSetQuery() Executes query OMOP vocabulary schema Returns results concept_id concept_name columns Extract Source Codes Concept Sets Vocabulary Suggestion Domain: function automatically suggests appropriate vocabularies based concept set domains: condition_occurrence: ICD10CM, ICD9CM procedure: HCPCS, CPT4 measurement: LOINC drug_exposure: NDC observation: vocabularies (ICD9CM, ICD10CM, HCPCS, CPT4, LOINC, NDC) device_exposure: NDC visit_occurrence: ICD10CM, ICD9CM, HCPCS, CPT4 Note: suggestions based OMOP CDM conventions. can override valid vocabulary combination. Processing Workflow: Verifies ExecutionSettings configured database connection Detects domains concept sets manifest Displays suggested vocabularies based detected domains Prompts user accept override suggested vocabularies Creates new xlsx workbook concept set manifest: Reads CIRCE JSON definition Builds concept query selecting standard concepts (using CirceR) Performs SQL join: concepts -> concept_relationship (Maps ) -> source concepts Finds matching source codes specified vocabularies Adds results new sheet xlsx workbook formatted header Provides status messages concept set Exports combined results {outputFolder}/SourceCodeWorkbook.xlsx sheet contains columns: vocabulary_id, concept_code, concept_name Sheet headers styled blue background white bold text Column widths auto-fitted readability SQL Query Pattern: concept set, following logic executed: CTE selects standard concepts concept set Joins concept_relationship table relationship_id = 'Maps ' Maps relationship finds source codes map standard concepts Filters valid, non-invalid source codes specified vocabularies Results ordered vocabulary_id concept_code Requirements: ExecutionSettings must initialized valid database connection Vocabulary schema must accessible ExecutionSettings openxlsx2 package must installed User must READ permissions vocabulary tables Error Handling: Displays warnings concept set processing fails continues others Provides clear error messages database connection unavailable Validates source vocabularies known vocabulary IDs function identifies standard concepts included concept set finding reverse mapping relationship. concept set: Reads CIRCE JSON definition Builds concept query using CirceR Joins concept_relationship via reverse "Maps " relationship (finds maps concept set concepts) Filters standard concepts (standard_concept = 'S') Adds results new sheet xlsx workbook Exports results {outputFolder}/IncludedCodes.xlsx sheet contains: concept_id, concept_name, vocabulary_id Requirements: ExecutionSettings must initialized valid connection Vocabulary schema must accessible ExecutionSettings openxlsx2 package must installed

title: picard 0.0.5
text: API CHANGES: loadCohortManifest() loadConceptSetManifest() now include autoSync (default TRUE) verbose (default TRUE) parameters controlling manifest initialization behavior resetCohortManifest() resetConceptSetManifest() now include archive parameter (default TRUE) create timestamped backups manifest database files reset importAtlasCohorts() importAtlasConceptSets() API CHANGE: changed file-based (conceptSetsLoadPath = "path//file.csv") dataframe-based (conceptSetsLoad = dataframe, atlasConnection = NULL). Users now call readr::read_csv() first, pass resulting dataframe. Dataframe must contain columns: atlasId, label, category (plus optional tag columns)

title: picard 0.0.5
text: Pre-Pipeline Input Loading System (sourceInputBuilderScripts(verbose = TRUE)): Auto-discovery sourcing builder scripts pre-processing pipeline Loads concept set definitions inputs/conceptSets/R/ directory (sourced first) Loads cohort definitions inputs/cohorts/R/ directory (sourced second) Called automatically main.R main pipeline executes Six Template Builder Scripts (auto-populated project init): Concept Set Builders: import_atlas_concept_set.R — bulk import ATLAS via CSV + WebAPI connection import_capr_concept_set.R — programmatic definition using Capr functions Cohort Builders: import_atlas_cohort.R — bulk import ATLAS via CSV + WebAPI connection import_capr_cohort.R — programmatic cohort definitions Capr import_sql_cohort.R — register hand-written SQL cohorts inputs/cohorts/sql/ build_dependent_cohorts.R — create derived cohorts (temporal, union, complement, O-Prior-T, T-Prior-O, censor) Mandatory Source Order ⚠️: Concept set builders (import_atlas_concept_set → import_capr_concept_set) Cohort builders (import_atlas_cohort → import_capr_cohort → import_sql_cohort → build_dependent_cohorts) ensures concept sets always loaded cohorts, base cohorts dependent cohorts Missing scripts gracefully skipped; deletion unused templates break main.R Users can delete unused scripts reorder sources

title: picard 0.0.5
text: Post-Pipeline Result Processing (sourceDisseminationScripts(projectPath, pipelineVersion, databaseIds, outputPath, verbose, warnMissing)): Auto-discovery sourcing post-processing scripts dissemination/pretty/R/ directory Called automatically runPostProcessing() completes main.R Scripts numbered (01_, 02_, etc.) sourced alphabetical order pipelineVersion — current pipeline version reproducibility databaseIds — vector databases included analysis outputPath — root output directory result export resultsPath — merged results file path post-processing Dissemination Script Creation (makeDisseminationScript(name = "format_results", projectPath, open = TRUE)): Template-based generation new dissemination scripts Auto-numbering: creates 01_name.R, 02_name.R, etc. based existing files Optional RStudio navigation newly created file Three-Phase Pipeline Integration: Phase 1: Pre-pipeline (builder scripts inputs/*/R/) Phase 2: Main execution (execStudyPipeline() runs analysis tasks) runPostProcessing() aggregates results sourceDisseminationScripts() runs numbered dissemination scripts formatting, pivoting, exporting

title: picard 0.0.5
text: Sync Methods ($syncManifest(strict_mode=TRUE)): CohortManifest ConceptSetManifest now include strict_mode parameter control strictness file/database reconciliation Reconciles files disk (json/, sql/) SQLite; flags missing files deleted, detects hash changes, reports unregistered files Integrated loadCohortManifest() loadConceptSetManifest() via autoSync parameter Update Methods (segmented field): $updateCohortLabel(), $updateCohortCategory(), $updateCohortTags() CohortManifest $updateConceptSetLabel(), $updateConceptSetCategory(), $updateConceptSetTags() ConceptSetManifest method updates single field syncs changes SQLite -memory manifest object methods return invisible NULL consistency R6 patterns

title: picard 0.0.5
text: Core Architecture: Every cohort/concept set SQLite manifest database maintains 1:1 correspondence exactly one definition file disk: - Circe/ATLAS cohorts: stored .json file json/ directory - SQL cohorts: stored .sql file sql/ directory - Derived cohorts: generated .sql files sql/derived/ directory dependency metadata SQLite Data Consistency Guarantees: record manifest file_path column pointing corresponding disk file File hashes stored SQLite compared $syncManifest() detect --sync changes Soft deletes (status = 'deleted') preserve database record file audit trail Hard deletes (performed) remove record file atomically Sync Integrity: $syncManifest(strict_mode=TRUE) enforces strict reconciliation: files disk must match database records Unregistered files disk flagged orphaned; missing files active records flagged missing autoSync=TRUE load functions ensures manifest synchronized disk startup 1:1 principle prevents silent data loss enables reliable recovery workflows

title: picard 0.0.5
text: Phase 1: Detection ($checkAtlasCohorts() / $checkAtlasConceptSets()): Read-operation: compares local hashes remote ATLAS definitions Returns tibble showing cohorts/concept sets changed remotely modifications local state; useful mid-cycle discovery Call parameters: atlasConnection = NULL (uses stored connection available) Phase 2: Update ($updateAtlasCohorts() / $updateAtlasConceptSets()): Downloads definition JSON files ATLAS cohorts/concept sets remote changes Updates JSON files disk json/ directory CohortManifest : automatically cascades stale status downstream derived cohorts depend updated base cohorts Derived cohorts automatically re-executed next generateCohorts() run without user intervention Workflow Automation: need separate “rebuild derived cohorts” step ATLAS updates Dependent cohort execution order automatically resolved via topological sort Users can chain checks updates seamlessly scripts: manifest$checkAtlasCohorts() → review results → manifest$updateAtlasCohorts()

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $addAtlasCohort(atlasId, label, category, tags, atlasConnection) — fetch single cohort JSON ATLAS register $addCaprCohort(caprCohort, label, category, tags) — export Capr Cohort object JSON register $addSqlCohort(filePath, label, category, tags) — register existing .sql file; validates portability $importAtlasCohorts(atlasConnection, cohortsLoadPath) — batch import via cohortsLoad.csv; extra columns become tags $setAtlasConnection(atlasConnection) / $getAtlasConnection() — store connection manifest need passed every call getAtlasConnection() now exported standalone function (replaces setAtlasConnection() deprecated)

title: picard 0.0.3
text: dependent cohort methods now CohortManifest class. Dependency metadata stored SQLite (depends_on, dependency_rule columns); sidecar JSON files. parent’s SQL file changes, downstream derived cohorts automatically marked stale. $buildUnionCohort(label, cohortIds, category, gapDays) — era-collapse union ≥2 cohorts $buildComplementCohort(label, populationCohortId, excludeCohortIds, category, complementType) — exclude subjects; updated signature; supports "exclude_any" (default) "exclude_all" $buildCompositeCohort(label, cohortIds, category, minCohorts) — intersection requiring membership ≥ minCohorts $buildSubsetCohortTemporal(label, baseCohortId, filterCohortId, category, startWindow, endWindow, endDateType, subsetLimit) — temporal subset using SubsetWindowOperator objects $buildDemographicCohort(label, baseCohortId, category, minAge, maxAge, genderConceptIds, raceConceptIds, ethnicityConceptIds) — new: filter base cohort person-level demographics $buildStratifiedCohorts(baseCohortId, strata, labelPrefix, category) — new: split cohort N named strata; automatically appends Unclassified stratum covering non-matching subjects Removed: addDependentCohort() (replaced entirely build*() methods )

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $tabulateManifest(filter) — tabulate manifest tibble; filter now accepts "active" (default), "deleted", "stale", "" $syncManifest() — reconcile files disk (json/, sql/) SQLite; flags missing files deleted, detects hash changes, reports unregistered files $reviewDependentCohorts() — new: tibble derived cohorts parsed parent labels rule summaries $reviewStaleCohorts() — new: list cohorts marked stale (parent SQL changed since last build); re-executed automatically executeCohortGeneration() $reloadFromDb() — new: refresh -memory manifest SQLite (useful external DB changes resetCohortManifest()) $statusReport() — tabular status overview active cohorts dependencies $validateManifest() — check file presence active cohort records $cleanupMissing(keep_trace) — soft-delete (keep_trace = TRUE) hard-remove (keep_trace = FALSE) cohorts whose files missing
